title: tpm — confluence_project_tracking_rods_support_for_dynamic_tables — v1
description: Synthesized by skill_builder for persona=tpm, kb=confluence_project_tracking_rods_support_for_dynamic_tables. Refine before promoting.

confluence_source_url.description: At query time, accept the Confluence page URL that is the single source of truth for this project/program tracking update; echo it back verbatim as the cited source link in the email.

project_name.description: Extract the project/program name from the page title and/or the 'Architecture / Product Definition' linked title; use the most human-readable name for the email subject and header.

meeting_name.description: If the page explicitly names a recurring meeting or forum, extract it; otherwise return an empty string (do not invent a meeting series name).

send_ahead_days.description: Capture the user-provided number of days ahead this email is framed as being sent before the meeting; if not provided at query time, set to 2.

meeting_datetime_local.description: If the page explicitly states a meeting date/time, extract it including timezone; otherwise return an empty string (do not infer from task dates).

timezone.description: If a timezone is explicitly stated on the page for dates/ETAs, extract it; otherwise return an empty string.

to_recipients.description: Derive the To: list by extracting stakeholder emails only when the page explicitly provides email addresses; otherwise return an empty array (do not guess distribution intent).

cc_recipients.description: Derive the Cc: list only if the page explicitly indicates a distribution list or secondary recipients; otherwise return an empty array.

orm_status.description: Extract ORM status only if the page contains an ORM ticket and/or explicit ORM status text; if the ORM field is a placeholder or absent, return 'Unknown (not specified on source page)'.

rag_summary.description: Derive a single exec-safe RAG (Red/Amber/Green) plus exactly one sentence justification by summarising: (1) WBS task statuses, (2) dated Task Status Updates, and (3) any Issues/Bug Tracking notes; avoid acronyms without expansion and do not list raw task-by-task detail.

schedule_health.description: Derive schedule health in 1–2 sentences by combining: (1) WBS due dates and milestone dates, (2) statements like 'must be delivered by' in updates, and (3) environment rollout timing notes; state whether the plan is on track, at risk, or slipping and why.

blockers_with_owners_and_etas.description: Derive the open blockers list by aggregating: (1) 'Issues encountered' / blockers language in Task Status Updates, (2) Bug Tracking items that are described as impacting progress, and (3) WBS rows that indicate dependency/blocked status. For each item, include: description, owner (person or team), target_resolution_date (ETA if stated), and critical_path (true if it blocks a dated milestone or 'must be delivered by' commitment; else false). If none, return an empty array.

dependencies.description: Extract and normalise dependencies by aggregating the WBS 'Dependency (FS, SS)' column and any dependency statements in Task Status Updates; output concise dependency statements with the dependent item and what it depends on (do not include internal shorthand without expansion).

per_stakeholder_status_rollcall.description: Derive a per-stakeholder roll-call by combining: (1) the Stakeholders roster, (2) WBS task owners, and (3) the most recent dated Task Status Updates mentioning each owner. For each stakeholder/owner, provide: name_or_team, current_status (1 line), next_commitment (what they will deliver), and eta_or_date (if stated; otherwise 'TBD'). Do not fabricate ETAs.

next_steps.description: Derive 3–7 time-bound next steps by summarising the most recent Task Status Updates and any remaining WBS items not marked complete; each step must include an owner and a due date ('by [date]' if stated, otherwise 'this sprint').

exec_asks.description: Derive executive asks by aggregating explicit requests in Task Status Updates (e.g., approvals, sign-offs, resourcing, cross-team escalations) and any statements that indicate a decision is needed; phrase each ask as a single sentence with the ask, the decision-maker/audience, and the needed-by date if stated. If none are explicit, return an empty array (do not invent asks).

reference_links.description: Extract key reference links from the page: Jira links, bug tracking links, test plan link, release/patch links, repository link, and architecture/design link; include each as {label, url}.

draft_eml.description: Compose a draft RFC-822 .eml email (plain text body) as meeting-prep sent N days ahead. Must include: Subject, To, Cc, and a structured agenda-style body with sections for ORM status, RAG summary (with 1-sentence justification), schedule health, blockers (with owners+ETAs), dependencies, per-stakeholder roll-call (owners+ETAs), next steps (time-bound), and exec asks. Include the Confluence source URL and key reference links. Draft only; never imply sending.

use_when.description: Use ONLY when the requested output is an email draft in RFC-822 .eml format (agenda-style meeting prep). NOT for slide decks, PowerPoint presentations, PPTX files, or any non-email agenda format.

do_not_invoke_if_phrases.description: Must include at least: 'slide deck', 'powerpoint', 'pptx', 'presentation', 'make a presentation'.

routing_queries.description: Update routing_queries.negative to include: (1) 'Make a slide deck agenda for the stakeholder status meeting based on the Confluence page.' (2) 'Create a PowerPoint presentation summarising the RODS Support project status from the Confluence tracking page.' (3) 'Build a deck for the stakeholder meeting that includes blockers and next steps from the Confluence tracker.'
